refsynopsisdiv.title: Syntax
refsynopsisdiv.synopsis: view = imshow3d(volume) slice = imshow3d(volume, sliceIndex) view = imshow3d(volume, mode) view = imshow3d(volume, sliceIndex, mode) view = imshow3d(volume, "3d", threshold, maxPoints)

listitem.para: Input 3-D scalar volume, indexed as volume(row, col, slice).
Optional slice index for axial display. The value is rounded and clipped to the valid slice range.
Optional intensity threshold for the 3-D voxel view. Voxels at or above this value are displayed.
Optional maximum number of voxels to plot in the 3-D view. Default is 12000.
Displayed raw slice, returned when sliceIndex is used without a display mode.
Structure containing the displayed slices and/or maximum-intensity projections.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: imshow3d is IPCV's volume display helper inspired by volume visualization workflows. The default mode renders a lightweight 3-D voxel point-cloud view from high-intensity voxels. Use the "volshow" mode or call volshow directly for interactive WebGL ray-cast volume rendering. The plotted coordinates use image columns as X, image rows as Y, and slices as Z; rows are flipped for display because image coordinates start at the upper-left corner while Scilab 3-D axes use a Cartesian view.
Use "overview" to show axial, coronal, and sagittal center slices together with maximum-intensity projections along the main volume directions.
This function expects a scalar 3-D volume. If a multipage image is read by imreadmulti as rows x cols x channels x pages, convert the page dimension to a 3-D volume first.

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.para: This example uses the public ImageJ MRI stack sample mri-stack.zip.

tr.td: 3-D voxel, volume-style overview, orthoslice, and MIP display modes added.
The volshow mode now opens the interactive GPU volume renderer.
